title: BG·1 Bergamo
wikitext: {{object |type_object=pot |material=pottery |dimension=ca. 6 x 2.9 cm (of fragment) |dimension_max=6 cm |condition=fragmentary |culture_archaeological=unknown |sortdate=0 |date=unknown |site=Bergamo |field_name=Piazza Mercato del Fieno |find_context=us 1/2 |coordinate_n=45.70419377601376 |coordinate_e=9.664492607116701 |sortdate_find=1981 |location=Civico Museo Archeologico (Bergamo) |inventory_number=A 5109, St 52441 |solinas=33 |morandi=211 |source_detail=Casini & Motta 2018: 118–120 no. 1 |checklevel=0 }} == Commentary == Images in {{bib|Solinas 1995}}: tav. LXV c (photo), {{bib|Morandi 2004}}: 656, fig. 24.211 (drawing), {{bib|Morandi 2007}}: 290, fig. 282 (photo), {{bib|Casini & Motta 2018}}: 119, fig. 2.2 (photo and drawings). Fragment of the shoulder, neck and rim of a pot; tapering outward-bending rim; fine micaceous clay; surface unglazed and orange-brown. Original rim diam. 7.6 cm. Found in the intersection of us 1 and 2 in a disturbed context together with materials from various phases. {{bib|Solinas 1995}}: 333, no. 33 and {{bib|Morandi 2004}}: 657, no. 211 (also {{bib|Morandi 2007}}: 290, no. 1) date the object to the 5th century BC (on palaeographical grounds?), but according to {{bib|Casini & Motta 2018}}: 118 it can be compared to similar objects from La Tène C1 and 2 from Milano (4th/3rd century BC). {{bib|Poggiani Keller 1982}}: 50 records the fragment as having come from stratum 2, dated to La Tène D.
